Collection 06

Diptychs & Triptychs

“One painting tells a story. Several paintings create a world.”

The Diptychs & Triptychs of contemporary artist Nona Dronova explore the expressive power of related compositions. Each painting is complete and independent, yet when combined with another work it gains new emotional depth and visual resonance. Colour, rhythm and movement continue from one canvas to the next, creating a broader artistic narrative than a single image can achieve.

Rather than repeating the same motif, each panel presents a different emotional perspective on the subject. Together they form a unified composition in which contrasts, echoes and subtle variations enrich the viewer’s experience. Flowers, light and colour unfold like chapters of the same visual poem, inviting the eye to move naturally across the sequence.

This artistic approach is reflected in several important series. The Hydrangeas collection expands a single botanical theme into multiple diptychs and even a long panoramic panel designed for placement above a bed or within contemporary interiors. In the lyrical series, compositions such as Goldenrod explore the quiet harmony of seasonal light, while expressive works such as Ferns demonstrate how dynamic brushwork and bold rhythms can unfold across several connected canvases. Each series illustrates how multiple paintings enrich one another while remaining complete works of art.

For Nona Dronova, the diptych and triptych are not simply formats but artistic methods that expand the possibilities of contemporary painting. Multiple canvases allow rhythm to become more dynamic, colour relationships more expressive and the emotional atmosphere more complete than could be achieved within a single composition.

“For Nona Dronova, a diptych or triptych is not the division of one image, but the expansion of one artistic idea into space. Each canvas remains independent while contributing to a larger visual dialogue that unfolds across the entire composition.”
